proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_057 == 2 have a lower probability of objective_response than patients with feature_057 == 0 (a monotonic decrease in response across the three feature_057 strata).
Patients with feature_035 == 1 have a higher probability of objective_response than patients with feature_035 == 0.
Patients with feature_093 == 1 have a higher probability of objective_response than patients with feature_093 == 0.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate by feature_057: 0 -> 0.213 (n=17592), 1 -> 0.155 (n=24971), 2 -> 0.112 (n=7437). Chi-square test of independence: chi2=447.9, p=5.51e-98. Monotonic decrease across categories supports an ordered/risk-like structure.
Response rate: feature_035=1 -> 0.221 (n=3624); feature_035=0 -> 0.165. Absolute rate difference +0.056, chi2 p=3.52e-18.
Response rate: feature_093=1 -> 0.185; feature_093=0 -> 0.168. Diff +0.017, chi2 p=5.64e-03.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_011 are associated with a lower probability of objective_response (negative association).
Higher values of feature_006 are associated with a lower probability of objective_response (negative association).
Higher values of feature_099 are associated with a higher probability of objective_response (positive association).
Higher values of feature_092 are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Higher values of feature_063 are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Logistic regression of objective_response on feature_011: beta=-0.0401 (per-unit log-odds), p=7.43e-35. Mean(feature_011 | response=1) - mean(feature_011 | response=0) = -0.569 (t-test p=7.20e-39).
Logistic regression of objective_response on feature_006: beta=-0.0057 (per-unit log-odds), p=1.16e-19. Mean(feature_006 | response=1) - mean(feature_006 | response=0) = -2.076 (t-test p=9.50e-20).
Logistic regression of objective_response on feature_099: beta=+0.1151 (per-unit log-odds), p=1.36e-06. Mean(feature_099 | response=1) - mean(feature_099 | response=0) = +0.029 (t-test p=1.30e-06).
Logistic regression of objective_response on feature_092: beta=-0.0022 (per-unit log-odds), p=1.88e-05. Mean(feature_092 | response=1) - mean(feature_092 | response=0) = -1.327 (t-test p=1.30e-05).
Logistic regression of objective_response on feature_063: beta=-0.0070 (per-unit log-odds), p=2.82e-06. Mean(feature_063 | response=1) - mean(feature_063 | response=0) = -0.512 (t-test p=4.37e-07).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Objective response rate differs across the levels of feature_005 (race/ethnicity), with white patients having a higher response rate than non-white patients overall.
Black patients have a lower objective_response rate than white patients.
Hispanic patients have a similar objective_response rate to white patients (no large group-level disparity).

analyses.result_summary: Response rates by feature_005: asian=0.163, black=0.157, hispanic=0.167, other=0.147, white=0.173. Chi-square test p=9.59e-03; effect (white minus mean of non-white) = +0.014.
Black 0.157 vs White 0.173; diff (black - white) = -0.015; chi2 p=4.00e-03.
Hispanic 0.167 vs White 0.173; diff (hispanic - white) = -0.006; chi2 p=2.26e-01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Objective_response rate differs across feature_087 insurance categories (medicare, medicaid, private, uninsured).
Uninsured patients have a lower objective_response rate than privately insured patients.

analyses.result_summary: Response rates: medicaid=0.169, medicare=0.172, private=0.167, uninsured=0.166. Chi-square p=5.40e-01 (range across groups = 0.0056).
Uninsured 0.166 vs Private 0.167; diff = -0.0004; chi2 p=0.990.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078 is unrelated to objective_response (no measurable association).
After adjusting for feature_057, feature_006 retains a negative association with objective_response (i.e., feature_006 is not merely a proxy for feature_057).

analyses.result_summary: Logistic beta on feature_078 = +0.00040 (p=0.718); mean diff = +0.047 (t-test p=0.72). No detectable association.
Adjusted for feature_057, feature_006 beta = -0.0057 (p=1.18e-19). Both feature_057 dummies remain significant (p<0.001). feature_006 retains independent negative association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 retains a negative association with objective_response after adjusting for feature_057, feature_006, feature_099, feature_092, feature_063, and feature_035.
feature_092 and feature_063 lose statistical significance once feature_011 is in the model (i.e., feature_011 captures their predictive content, suggesting collinearity among them).
feature_099 retains a positive association with objective_response after adjustment for the other top predictors.

analyses.code: logit('resp ~ feature_011 + feature_006 + feature_099 + feature_092 + feature_063 + feature_035 + C(feature_057)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logit (resp ~ feature_011 + feature_006 + feature_099 + feature_092 + feature_063 + feature_035 + C(feature_057)): C(feature_057)[T.1]: beta=-0.3833, p=4.35e-51; C(feature_057)[T.2]: beta=-0.7661, p=6.43e-77; feature_011: beta=-0.0403, p=1.32e-34; feature_006: beta=-0.0057, p=6.90e-20; feature_099: beta=+0.1180, p=9.34e-07; feature_092: beta=-0.0022, p=1.04e-05; feature_063: beta=-0.0073, p=1.32e-06; feature_035: beta=+0.3597, p=1.89e-17.

analyses.code: extract feature_092 coef from same model
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_092 beta = -0.00224 (p=0.000); feature_063 beta = -0.00727 (p=0.000). Both substantially attenuated relative to univariate.

analyses.code: extract feature_099 coef from same model
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_099 beta = +0.1180 (p=9.34e-07).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011, feature_092, and feature_063 are positively correlated with each other (a single underlying clinical construct, e.g. a tumor-burden composite).
The negative effect of feature_011 on objective_response is stronger (more negative slope) in patients with feature_057 == 2 than in feature_057 == 0 (interaction effect).

analyses.result_summary: Main feature_011 beta (at feature_057=0) = -0.0410 (p=4.95e-16). Interaction terms: feature_011:C(feature_057)[T.1]: beta=+0.0045, p=0.520; feature_011:C(feature_057)[T.2]: beta=-0.0109, p=0.345. Effect reported is the incremental slope of feature_011 in feature_057=2 vs 0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_035 == 1 (higher response) is heterogeneous across feature_057 strata; specifically, the absolute response advantage is larger in feature_057 == 0 than in feature_057 == 2.

analyses.result_summary: Stratified rates: feature_057=0: feature_035=1 rate 0.270 vs 0 rate 0.208 (diff +0.061, n_pos=1331, p=1.66e-07); feature_057=1: feature_035=1 rate 0.205 vs 0 rate 0.151 (diff +0.053, n_pos=1777, p=2.45e-09); feature_057=2: feature_035=1 rate 0.153 vs 0 rate 0.109 (diff +0.044, n_pos=516, p=2.63e-03). Logit interaction model: feature_035 main effect (at feature_057=0) beta=+0.3396 (p=1.54e-07); interaction terms feature_035:C(feature_057)[T.1]: beta=+0.0279, p=0.754; feature_035:C(feature_057)[T.2]: beta=+0.0530, p=0.712.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within privately insured patients, response rate does not differ meaningfully across feature_005 (race/ethnicity) levels (i.e. the small overall race differences are not driven by an insurance-mediated pathway).
Within Medicare beneficiaries, white patients have a slightly higher response rate than black patients.

analyses.code: chi2 by race within private insurance
analyses.result_summary: Within private: asian=0.189, black=0.142, hispanic=0.163, other=0.146, white=0.170. Chi-square p=0.002.

analyses.code: chi2 white vs black within medicare
analyses.result_summary: Within medicare, white 0.175 vs black 0.171; diff (white - black) = +0.0047; p=0.590.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for feature_057, feature_006, feature_011, and feature_099, the apparent advantage of white patients (feature_005=='white') versus non-white patients in objective_response is attenuated and no longer statistically significant (i.e., the unadjusted race difference is largely confounded by clinical/risk variables).

analyses.code: logit before/after adjustment for clinical covariates
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted: nonwhite beta = -0.0779 (p=0.002). Adjusted for feature_057 + feature_006 + feature_011 + feature_099: nonwhite beta = -0.0751 (p=0.003).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The relationship between feature_006 and objective_response is approximately linear on the log-odds scale (a quadratic term in feature_006 does not significantly improve fit).

analyses.result_summary: Linear logit: feature_006 beta=-0.0057 (p=1.16e-19). Adding feature_006^2: beta_sq=-0.00001 (p=0.722); LR test chi2=0.13, p=0.722.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_011 == 0 (no detectable level) have a higher objective_response rate than patients with feature_011 > 0.
Among patients with feature_011 > 0, higher feature_011 is still associated with lower response (i.e., the negative association is not solely explained by a zero-vs-nonzero effect).
The negative effect of feature_006 on response does not differ substantially across feature_057 strata (no significant interaction).

analyses.code: LR test of feature_006 x feature_057 interaction
analyses.result_summary: Interaction terms: feature_006:C(feature_057)[T.1]: beta=-0.00030, p=0.820; feature_006:C(feature_057)[T.2]: beta=-0.00256, p=0.238. Joint LR test for interaction (df=2): chi2=1.42, p=0.491.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for feature_057, feature_006, feature_011, and feature_099, insurance status (feature_087) has no detectable independent association with objective_response.

analyses.code: LR test of insurance after clinical covariate adjustment
analyses.result_summary: Insurance terms: C(feature_087)[T.medicare]: beta=+0.0222, p=0.551; C(feature_087)[T.private]: beta=-0.0163, p=0.665; C(feature_087)[T.uninsured]: beta=-0.0117, p=0.863. Joint LR test (df=3): chi2=2.17, p=0.537.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_121 == 1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with feature_121 == 0.
Patients with feature_014 == 1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with feature_014 == 0.
feature_084 has at most a weak association with objective_response, with higher values weakly associated with higher response, that does not survive adjustment for feature_057 and feature_006.

analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted feature_084 beta=+0.00047 (p=0.048); adjusted beta=+0.00054 (p=0.025).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The race difference in objective_response (white vs non-white) does not vary significantly across feature_057 strata (no race x feature_057 interaction).

analyses.code: LR test for nonwhite x feature_057 interaction
analyses.result_summary: Interaction terms: nonwhite:C(feature_057)[T.1]: beta=+0.0125, p=0.816; nonwhite:C(feature_057)[T.2]: beta=-0.0735, p=0.402. Joint LR test: chi2=0.99, p=0.610.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a full multivariable logit including feature_057, feature_006, feature_011, feature_099, feature_035, feature_005, and feature_087, only the clinical features (feature_057 levels, feature_006, feature_011, feature_099, feature_035) remain statistically significant, while race and insurance are not independently associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: logit('resp ~ feature_006 + feature_011 + feature_099 + feature_035 + C(feature_057) + C(feature_005) + C(feature_087)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Full model: C(feature_057)[T.1]: beta=-0.3828, p=5.74e-51; C(feature_057)[T.2]: beta=-0.7649, p=9.99e-77; C(feature_005)[T.black]: beta=-0.0284, p=6.46e-01; C(feature_005)[T.hispanic]: beta=+0.0424, p=4.72e-01; C(feature_005)[T.other]: beta=-0.1164, p=2.56e-01; C(feature_005)[T.white]: beta=+0.0791, p=1.29e-01; C(feature_087)[T.medicare]: beta=+0.0218, p=5.59e-01; C(feature_087)[T.private]: beta=-0.0203, p=5.89e-01; C(feature_087)[T.uninsured]: beta=-0.0121, p=8.58e-01; feature_006: beta=-0.0057, p=9.18e-20; feature_011: beta=-0.0401, p=2.14e-34; feature_099: beta=+0.1180, p=9.12e-07; feature_035: beta=+0.3603, p=1.64e-17.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction such that the negative association of feature_011 with response is attenuated in patients with higher feature_099 (feature_099 buffers the adverse effect of feature_011).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = +0.00806 (p=0.213). LR chi2=1.55, p=0.213.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_035 == 1 (higher response) is preserved across the range of feature_011 (no significant feature_035 x feature_011 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -0.0140 (p=0.225); LR chi2=1.49, p=0.223.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the remaining unexplored binary features, none have a stronger association with objective_response than the previously identified top predictors (feature_057, feature_035, feature_093).

analyses.result_summary: Top 5 second-tier features: feature_012: effect=-0.0067, p=5.07e-02; feature_033: effect=-0.0387, p=5.22e-02; feature_111: effect=-0.0108, p=5.68e-02; feature_031: effect=+0.0038, p=5.77e-02; feature_030: effect=+0.0144, p=6.43e-02. None remain significant after Bonferroni correction across 110 tests (alpha=4.55e-04).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is no significant race (feature_005) by insurance (feature_087) interaction on objective_response, beyond their (small) main effects.

analyses.code: LR test of race x insurance interaction
analyses.result_summary: Race x insurance interaction LR test: chi2=22.92, df=12, p=0.028.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the fully adjusted model the strongest independent predictors of objective_response (in order of effect magnitude) are: higher feature_057 (negative), higher feature_011 (negative), higher feature_006 (negative), higher feature_099 (positive), and feature_035 == 1 (positive).

Sociodemographic features (feature_005, feature_087) show no clinically meaningful disparities in objective_response after accounting for clinical covariates, indicating measured outcome disparities by race/insurance in this dataset are largely confounded by clinical risk.

analyses.result_summary: Ranked by |beta|: C(feature_057)[T.2]: beta=-0.7649 p=9.99e-77; C(feature_057)[T.1]: beta=-0.3828 p=5.74e-51; feature_035: beta=+0.3603 p=1.64e-17; feature_099: beta=+0.1180 p=9.12e-07; C(feature_005)[T.other]: beta=-0.1164 p=2.56e-01; C(feature_005)[T.white]: beta=+0.0791 p=1.29e-01; C(feature_005)[T.hispanic]: beta=+0.0424 p=4.72e-01; feature_011: beta=-0.0401 p=2.14e-34.

analyses.code: joint LR test of race+insurance after clinical adjustment
analyses.result_summary: Joint LR test for race+insurance after clinical adjustment: chi2=15.79, df=7, p=0.027.
